analysis_waves: Wave 1-20. loop_d_1 through loop_d_286. Total: 425+ entries.

raw_retention_at_20s.description: THE STRONGEST SINGLE PREDICTOR: raw retention at the 20-second mark. If 80% of viewers are still watching at 20 seconds, the video will perform. This is a stronger predictor than any above-baseline metric.
raw_retention_at_20s.design_rule: The first 20 seconds are EVERYTHING. If viewers survive to 20s, they watch the whole thing. All content in 0-20s must be engineered to prevent dropout.

top_5_retention_predictors.description: The gap between hook above_baseline and payoff above_baseline. NEGATIVE correlation = over-delivery wins. Videos where the ending exceeds the hook promise get 9x more views.
top_5_retention_predictors.design_rule: The ending MUST exceed the opening promise. Never frontload your best content. Build to a climax that surpasses the hook.

top_5_retention_predictors.description: Above-baseline retention at 80-95% of the video. The single strongest zone predictor. Q4 videos average +0.055 here while Q1 averages -0.089.
top_5_retention_predictors.design_rule: Save the biggest payoff for the final 15%. The ending matters 8.5x more than the hook for total views.

top_5_retention_predictors.description: Longest sustained run of above-baseline retention. Mean run = 50 points. % time above baseline also r=+0.47. Sustained quality > dramatic peaks.
top_5_retention_predictors.design_rule: Consistency beats spikes. One long above-baseline run is worth more than multiple isolated peaks.

top_5_retention_predictors.description: Later drops are 2.15x steeper than early drops (-0.129 vs -0.060). And late drops are almost never recovered (3/472). Position × severity interaction.
top_5_retention_predictors.design_rule: A drop at 70% is fatal. A drop at 20% can be recovered. Front-load any risky content.

top_5_retention_predictors.description: MORE retention peaks = FEWER views. Fewer, bigger dramatic moments > many small ones. Peak/drop ratio also inversely predicts (r=-0.33).
top_5_retention_predictors.design_rule: Don't try to pack in multiple peaks. Build one clear narrative arc with one major payoff.

top_3_retention_peak_causes.evidence: At best above_baseline moments: action frames = 28% (vs 8% at worst). reaction→wide transitions 1.95x enriched at peaks. GPT-4o-mini confirms emotion slightly higher at peaks (2.78 vs 2.72).
top_3_retention_peak_causes.design_rule: Every 5-10 seconds, show physical action or an impact/reveal. Reaction shots followed by wide reveals are the strongest transition at peaks.

top_3_retention_peak_causes.evidence: Word impact scores: painful(+0.059), curious(+0.061), numb(+0.050), stomach(+0.051), skin(+0.046), feeling(+0.037). Sensory rate is the strongest transcript feature for predicting retention (weight=+1.59). Tension words 1.32x enriched at peaks vs drops.
top_3_retention_peak_causes.design_rule: Describe experiences through the body: 'this HURTS', 'my hands are freezing', 'I can feel my legs shaking'. Sensory language is the single best transcript predictor of retention.

top_3_retention_peak_causes.cause: SLOWER SPEAKING SPEED AT KEY MOMENTS
top_3_retention_peak_causes.evidence: Speaking speed at peaks: 3.86 w/s vs drops: 4.26 w/s (p=0.0003). Neutral speed is 4.44 w/s. Slowing down at dramatic moments lets the viewer absorb the impact.
top_3_retention_peak_causes.effect_size: 0.40 w/s slower at peaks vs drops
top_3_retention_peak_causes.design_rule: Slow down at key moments. Let the impact breathe. Speed up during setup/transitions but deliver payoffs at a deliberate pace.

top_3_retention_drop_causes.evidence: Word impact scores: plastic(-0.171), solid(-0.163), fiber(-0.138), materials(-0.136), carbon(-0.109), built(-0.095), metal(-0.095). Drop-exclusive words: 'plate', 'chest', 'magnets', 'laser'. These are 14x overrepresented at drops.
top_3_retention_drop_causes.design_rule: NEVER name materials. Show the result, not the process. 'Watch what happens' > 'I used carbon fiber reinforced with graphene layers'.

top_3_retention_drop_causes.cause: FACE + TEXT WITHOUT ACTION (talking head trap)
top_3_retention_drop_causes.evidence: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.
top_3_retention_drop_causes.effect_size: Face 0.31 higher at drops, Text 0.33 higher at drops
top_3_retention_drop_causes.design_rule: Never have Tyler just talking to camera with text overlays. If his face is on screen, he must be DOING something physical.

top_3_retention_drop_causes.evidence: Viewer fatigue r=-0.31: later drops 2.15x steeper. Only 3/472 late drops were recovered. Once viewers start leaving in the back half, they don't come back.
top_3_retention_drop_causes.design_rule: Never put boring content after 60%. Any setup/explanation must happen in the first 30%. The back half must be pure payoff.

best_arc.description: 54% of all videos follow this arc. Consistently building above_baseline from hook to end.

second_best.description: Strong mid-section peak with good hook and end. Classic narrative arc.

worst_arc.description: Front-loaded content that loses viewers progressively. 10x worse than steady_rise.

narrative_arc_analysis.design_rule: Build upward. Every segment should be more engaging than the last. Never peak in the first third.

consistently_above.description: Above baseline throughout — hook, mid, and end all positive

weak_start_strong_end.description: Starts slightly below, builds through. Most common shape (50%).

below_baseline_mild.description: Moderately below baseline. 10x fewer views than consistently_above.

below_baseline_deep.description: Deeply below baseline throughout. 20x fewer views.

quartile_comparison.description: How top 25% vs bottom 25% videos differ in above_baseline curve
quartile_comparison.key_insight: Hook gap is only 0.01 (negligible). End gap is 0.144 (massive). The ending separates 200K from 22M views. The body-to-end progression is what matters, not the hook.

consecutive_event_patterns.peak_to_drop_amplification: Drops after peaks are 22% larger (-0.061 vs -0.050). A peak sets expectations higher, making the subsequent drop more severe.
consecutive_event_patterns.drop_to_drop_compounding: Consecutive drops compound at -0.050 average. Once dropping, it gets worse.
consecutive_event_patterns.design_rule: After a peak moment, immediately follow with another peak or maintain energy. Never let a peak be followed by a lull.

word_impact_dictionary.pattern: Body sensation words retain. Material/technical words kill. The gap is 3x larger for negative words — avoiding bad words matters more than using good ones.

opening_words.design_rule: Start with ACTION or CHALLENGE words, not self-reference. End with IMPACT words. 'Go' > 'I'. 'Insane' > 'subscribe'.

speaking_patterns.speed_at_events: Peaks: 3.86 w/s, Drops: 4.26 w/s, Neutral: 4.44 w/s (p=0.0003)
speaking_patterns.repetition_rate: Mean 30.6%. Higher repetition mildly correlates with views (r=+0.16). Familiar words are comforting, not boring.
speaking_patterns.pauses: Pauses hurt retention (ab=-0.060 during pauses vs -0.016 during speech). Keep talking. Pause rate r=-0.18 vs retention.
speaking_patterns.opening_density: 3.9 words/sec average in first 3s. Medium density (1.5-3.0 w/s) performs best (6.39 avg views).

neg_to_pos.description: Starting negative, ending positive — the comeback arc

pos_to_neg.description: Starting positive, ending negative — the disappointment arc

emotional_trajectory.Q3_sensory: Sensory words in Q3 (40-60%) correlate with Q3 retention r=+0.16 (p=0.002)
emotional_trajectory.cumulative_tension: Tension word accumulation positively correlates with above_baseline (r=+0.23). 63% of videos show positive correlation.
emotional_trajectory.design_rule: Build tension. Start with a problem, escalate through body/sensory language, resolve at the end. The comeback arc (neg→pos) averages 10x more views than the disappointment arc.

cross_modal_alignment.verbal_only_best: Verbal-only energy moments: ab=+0.009 (best of all categories)
cross_modal_alignment.key_insight: Verbal energy is MORE important than visual energy. Words drive retention more than frames. But combining both is second-best.

vision_analysis_gpt4o_mini.key_insight: Face is MORE visible at drops than peaks in high-view videos. The 'talking head + text overlay' combination is a drop trigger. Face + action = peak. Face + explanation = drop.

transcript_prediction_model.note: Transcript features alone explain 13.7% of retention variance. Confirms sensory_rate and tension_rate as key script levers.

final_predictor_model_wave8.note: Combined curve + transcript features. CV R²=0.391 means we can explain ~39% of view variance. Q1=286K vs Q4=6.9M (24x gap).

hook_payoff.description: Ending above_baseline minus hook above_baseline. Over-delivery is the #1 predictor.

curve_slope.description: Overall slope of the retention curve. NEGATIVE because steeper upward slope = already captured in other features.

log_dur.description: Log duration. Longer videos tend to get more views (within Shorts range).

climax.description: Mean above_baseline at 80-95%. The ending zone value.

pct_above.description: Percentage of time spent above baseline. Consistency.

compound_score_v2.description: Combines climax, hook_payoff, early_momentum, pct_above, smoothness, sensory_rate, payoff_rate, material_rate, CTA_rate, and duration.

duration_insight.sweet_spot: 50-55s maximizes views. 40-55s is the safe range.

retention_smoothness.description: Smoother curves = more views. Q1 jagged=800K vs Q4 smooth=2.5M (3.1x gap).
retention_smoothness.design_rule: Avoid jagged retention — maintain steady engagement without sharp spikes and drops.

STP_arc.description: Sensory→Tension→Payoff ordering. Perfect arc avg=6.54 (3.4M) vs no arc avg=6.11 (1.3M).
STP_arc.design_rule: Structure scripts: start with body/sensory setup, build tension through the middle, resolve with payoff at the end.

payoff_density_finale.description: Concentrating payoff words (finally, done, worked, survived) in the last 20% correlates with views.
payoff_density_finale.design_rule: Save resolution language for the final 20%. Don't reveal outcomes too early.

early_momentum_predicts_end.description: The slope of retention from 10-30% predicts both the ending value AND views. What happens in the first 15s sets the trajectory.
early_momentum_predicts_end.design_rule: The first 15 seconds after the hook must GAIN momentum. If the curve is flat or declining by 30%, the video is already in trouble.

curve_signature.description: Climax(r=0.506) + early_momentum(r=0.357) + total_above(r=0.458) combined score. Q1=442K vs Q4=3.86M (8.7x gap).

retention_cliffs.description: Cliff-free videos avg 2.13M vs cliff videos 1.10M. Avoiding sharp drops is critical.
retention_cliffs.design_rule: Never create a retention cliff (>5% drop in 3 normalized points). Smooth transitions always.

duration_x_arc_interaction.design_rule: If the curve rises, go long (55s+). If the curve declines, duration won't save it.

quartile_templates.key_insight: Q4 starts slightly below baseline then climbs. Q1 starts slightly above then declines. The ENDING gap is 14.6% at the 94% mark. Hooks are nearly identical.

derivative_zones.strongest: Zone 0-10% derivative r=-0.492 (the initial drop speed predicts everything)
derivative_zones.note: The first 10% trajectory is the STRONGEST single zone predictor. Fast initial drop = fewer views.

word_position_matrix.material_words: Toxic at ALL positions (-0.05 to -0.14 above_baseline)
word_position_matrix.emotion_at_D9: +0.069 (emotion words are most powerful at the very end)
word_position_matrix.payoff_at_D9: +0.026 (payoff words peak at the end, negative everywhere else)

negative_AUC.description: The area BELOW baseline matters more than the area above. Minimizing the depth of bad moments is more predictive than maximizing good moments.

progression_patterns.design_rule: Every quarter must be at least as good as the last. A single declining quarter is acceptable if the others rise.

vision_expanded.key_insight: Reaction frames dominate both events but 1.7x more at drops. At drops, Tyler is reacting to/explaining something. At peaks, there is more action.

worst_moment_timing.description: Top quartile videos have worst moment at 17%. Bottom quartile at 49%. Successful videos front-load their worst content where it's recoverable.
worst_moment_timing.design_rule: Put your weakest/setup content in the first 20%. Never let the worst moment happen after 30%.

recovery_speed.design_rule: If there's a dip, bounce back FAST. The speed of recovery after the nadir is a strong predictor.

best_after_worst.design_rule: Structure: low point → high point. Never reverse. The nadir must come before the climax.

utterance_length_at_events.design_rule: Keep sentences SHORT at high-energy moments. Long explanations cause drops.

divergence_point.first_significant: 22% — where top/bottom quartiles begin to separate
divergence_point.max_divergence: 94% (+14.6% gap) — where the separation is maximal
divergence_point.design_rule: By 22% of the video, the fate is being determined. The gap only grows from there.

word_velocity_confirmed.design_rule: CONFIRMED: speak slower at climactic moments. 3.0 w/s at peaks vs 3.8 at drops.

effect_sizes.note: Cohen's d between top 50 and bottom 50 videos. pct_above and climax dominate.

golden_ratio_timing.design_rule: Best moment should be LATE (70%+ of video). Closer to 61.8% golden ratio = more views.

diminishing_returns.zero_is_worst: Videos with 0% of any category word get ~313K-656K views. ANY amount of sensory/action/challenge helps.
diminishing_returns.challenge_optimal: Q3 (0.29%) — moderate challenge language is best
diminishing_returns.design_rule: Some is essential. Zero category words is the kiss of death. But more is not always better.

scorecard_system.description: 7-dimension 0-10 scorecard. Total score correlates r=0.545 with views. Saved to video-scorecards.json.

survival_rates.design_rule: Survival at 90% is the best raw retention predictor (r=0.505). Early loss rate (25→50%) is the worst sign (r=-0.44).

design_rules_summary: 1. END > HOOK: The ending matters 8.5x more than the hook. Save your best content for the final 15%.
2. OVER-DELIVER: The payoff must EXCEED the hook promise. Under-delivery = 9x fewer views. hook_payoff is the #1 predictor in the final model (coef=+0.68).
3. BODY LANGUAGE: Use sensory words (painful, numb, freezing, sweat). Avoid materials (plastic, fiber, carbon, metal). Material words toxic at ALL positions.
4. STEADY BUILD: ↑↑↑ progression = 4.2M views. ↓↓↓ = 222K. 19x gap. Every quarter must build.
5. WORST MOMENT EARLY: Top videos have their worst moment at 17%, bottom at 49%. Front-load weak content. Best AFTER worst = 5x more views.
6. SLOW THE PAYOFF: 3.0 w/s at peaks vs 3.8 at drops vs 4.4 neutral. Short utterances (7.9w) at peaks, long (25.3w) at drops.
7. NEVER DROP LATE: A retention drop after 60% is 2.15x steeper and almost unrecoverable. Recovery speed r=0.398 (fast=4.8M vs none=510K).
8. LATE CLIMAX: Best moment at 70%+ = 3.23M vs <30% = 627K (5.2x). Distance from golden ratio (61.8%) r=-0.415.
9. SMOOTH CURVES: Retention smoothness r=0.247 (d=0.96). No cliffs (>5% drop). Jagged=800K vs smooth=2.5M.
10. EARLY MOMENTUM: Slope from 10-30% predicts both ending (r=0.44) and views (r=0.43). Divergence starts at 22%.
11. STP ARC: Sensory→Tension→Payoff. Perfect ordering = 3.4M, no ordering = 1.3M.
12. GO LONG + RISE: Long(55s+) + rising curve = 5.5M. Short + falling = 368K. 50-55s = sweet spot (2.88M avg).
13. MINIMIZE NEGATIVE: Negative AUC (r=0.42) matters more than positive. Avoiding bad moments > maximizing good ones.
14. INITIAL TRAJECTORY: Zone 0-10% derivative r=-0.49. Don't lose viewers in the first 10%. Survival @90% = r=0.505.
15. SOME CATEGORY WORDS: Zero sensory/action/challenge = worst (313K-656K). Any amount of category language helps. Optimal sensory ~0.01%, tension ~2.3%, challenge ~0.3%.
16. SHAPE+DURATION: '++++_long' = 12.6M (above baseline + long). '----_short' = 176K. 71x gap from shape × duration alone.
17. END>BEGIN RATIO: End stronger than beginning = 4.7M vs begin stronger = 398K (11.8x). r=0.293.
18. SUSTAINED STREAK: Above-baseline streak of 50+ points = 3.58M vs <20 = 561K. Sustained quality > peaks.
19. SIMPLE VOCABULARY: Lower word diversity (type-token ratio) = more views (r=-0.216). Use simple, repeated words.
20. PREDICTION CEILING: Content signals explain ~27% of view variance (CV R²=0.271). ~73% is external (algorithm, thumbnails, timing).

shape_encoding.note: Starting neutral then rising BEATS being above baseline the whole time.

word_diversity.description: Lower type-token ratio = more views. Simple, repeated vocabulary outperforms rich vocabulary.

hook_taxonomy.note: Stakes-based hooks underperform mystery and transformation hooks.

sub_nonsub_divergence.description: Bigger sub-nonsub retention gap = FEWER views. Viral videos retain both audiences equally. Sub fraction is the strongest single predictor (r=-0.86): more non-subscriber views = more total views.
sub_nonsub_divergence.design_rule: Content that appeals equally to subscribers and non-subscribers goes viral. Don't make inside-joke content.

velocity_shape.description: Algorithm rewards sustained growth. Spike-and-die videos (866K) are 4.5x worse than slow builds (3.9M).
velocity_shape.design_rule: Don't chase day-1 spikes. Videos that build slowly over 14 days get 4.5x more total views.

curve_asymmetry.design_rule: Back-loaded retention curves (building to the end) get 6.7x more views than front-loaded ones.

velocity_x_retention.description: Slow velocity + good retention = the algorithm's favorite. Fast starts with bad retention = the algorithm quickly stops pushing.
velocity_x_retention.design_rule: Early velocity doesn't help if retention is bad. Focus on retention quality; the algorithm will find the audience.

swipe_x_retention.design_rule: Low swipe rate + strong ending is the ideal signal to the algorithm.

concept_intro_timing.design_rule: Mention your main concept in the FIRST 10% of the video. Delaying it past 30% costs 5x views.

triple_quadrant.description: The 3 dimensions that matter: (1) non-subscriber audience fraction, (2) retention quality, (3) velocity shape. Non-sub fraction is the strongest.
triple_quadrant.design_rule: The ultimate viral formula: high non-sub reach + good retention + slow sustained growth.

monotonicity.description: Fraction of rising steps in the smoothed curve. More monotonically rising = more views.
monotonicity.design_rule: Never let the curve dip. Every segment must be at least as good as the previous one. Monotonically rising = 7.2x more views.

anchor_points.description: The YouTube algorithm appears to heavily weight the 80-95% retention zone. This is the single strongest predictor zone.
anchor_points.design_rule: The 80-95% zone of your video is what the algorithm evaluates most. This is where your best content must be.

zone_transition_speed.description: The rate of improvement in the 25-50% zone is the strongest transition speed predictor. Accelerating through setup into the middle is critical.
zone_transition_speed.design_rule: Don't linger in setup. Accelerate the retention curve upward through the 25-50% zone.

speech_gaps.description: Surprisingly, above_baseline during speech gaps (>0.5s) strongly predicts views (r=0.425). Videos with higher retention even during pauses are winners.
speech_gaps.design_rule: Pauses are acceptable IF the visual content holds attention. Pair silence with compelling visuals.

volatility_10_20.description: Volatility in the 10-20% window is the most harmful. Unstable early engagement kills videos.
volatility_10_20.design_rule: Keep the 10-20% zone SMOOTH. No sudden changes. Establish a clear trajectory early.

final_integrated_model.description: 13-feature ridge regression explains 58% of view variance in cross-validation. Sub fraction is the dominant feature — it's largely a proxy for algorithmic reach.

final_3s_content.design_rule: Top videos end with impact words (insane, days) and forward momentum (next, after). Bottom videos end with self-reference (your, profile) and pointing elsewhere (here, already).

design_rules_summary_v3: 1. AUDIENCE REACH > EVERYTHING: Non-subscriber fraction r=-0.87 with sub_frac. Making content that reaches BEYOND your subscribers is the #1 predictor. Don't make niche/inside content.
2. END > HOOK: 80-95% zone is r=0.505 anchor. Back-loaded curves = 3.9M vs front-loaded = 580K (6.7x). Save the best for last.
3. OVER-DELIVER: Hook-payoff gap remains top regression feature. Ending must EXCEED the opening promise.
4. MONOTONIC RISE: r=0.428 — every segment must improve. Q4=3.5M vs Q1=485K (7.2x). No dips allowed.
5. SLOW BUILD VELOCITY: slow_build=3.9M vs spike_and_die=866K. Algorithm rewards sustained 14-day growth, not day-1 spikes.
6. CONCEPT EARLY: Mention main concept in first 10% (2.6M) vs after 30% (476K). 5.4x gap.
7. BODY LANGUAGE: Sensory words retain. Material words kill at ALL positions. Gap is 3x larger for negatives.
8. SMOOTH 10-20% ZONE: Volatility here is most harmful (r=-0.31). Establish trajectory without wobbling.
9. SETUP→MID ACCELERATION: Speed of improvement in 25-50% zone is the strongest transition predictor (r=0.44).
10. SWIPE + END COMBO: low_swipe+strong_end=5.6M vs high_swipe+weak_end=493K (11.3x).
11. SLOW PAYOFF DELIVERY: 3.0 w/s at peaks vs 4.4 neutral. Short sentences at climax (7.9 words).
12. GO LONG + RISE: 50-55s sweet spot. Long + rising = 5.5M. Short + falling = 368K.
13. MINIMIZE NEGATIVE AUC: Avoiding bad moments matters more than maximizing good ones.
14. TRIPLE FORMULA: non-sub reach + good retention + slow build = 8.2M. The opposite = 201K (40.8x).
15. PREDICTION MODEL: 13-feature model explains 58% of view variance (CV R²=0.58). 42% is external.
